case_summary: Earth-leakage breaker on the DB board trips intermittently, especially in wet weather.

ground_truth.notes: Classic DB-board earth-leakage trip pattern (water ingress somewhere on the circuit). Tests routing to db_board_tripping rather than load_shedding_surge or lights_wiring.

inputs.user_text: The big switch on the left of our DB board keeps tripping out — we think it's the earth leakage. It happens randomly through the day but it's much worse when it rains. When it trips the whole house goes dark and we have to flip it back up. Sometimes it stays on for hours, sometimes only minutes.
